assertions.title: OAuth callback escapes HTML in error_description

steps.expected: Angle brackets entity-encoded as <script>, not raw HTML
steps.observed:
Response body contains

<script>alert('xss')</script>

— all angle brackets and quotes are entity-encoded

assertions.title: All five HTML-significant characters are escaped

steps.observed:
Response body contains

<>&"'

— all five characters correctly escaped

steps.expected: Entity-encoded in HTML source but would render as readable text in browser
steps.observed:
Response body contains

O'Brien & Associates

— correctly entity-encoded, no double-encoding. A browser renders this as 'O'Brien & Associates'

assertions.title: Static error strings are not double-encoded

steps.expected: Response contains 'Missing OAuth callback state or code.' verbatim with no encoding artifacts
steps.observed:
Response body contains

Missing OAuth callback state or code.

— the string appears verbatim with no & or other encoding artifacts

assertions.title: Both OAuth callback routes are protected

steps.expected: Same XSS payload escaped identically on second route
steps.observed: Response contains <script>alert('xss')</script> — identical escaping to first route

steps.expected: Static error message without encoding artifacts
steps.observed: Response contains 'Missing OAuth callback state or code.' verbatim — same behavior as /auth/callback

assertions.title: Exception messages in catch blocks are escaped

steps.expected: Error thrown by completeBrowserFlow is passed through htmlError() which calls escapeHtml()
steps.observed:
Response contains

Unknown or expired OAuth state

— confirms catch block path reached and uses htmlError()

steps.action: Code inspection of catch block at ui-routes.ts:709-711
steps.expected: catch block uses htmlError() which calls escapeHtml()
steps.observed: Line 711: reply.send(htmlError(oauthError instanceof Error ? oauthError.message : String(oauthError))) — confirmed htmlError() wraps all exception messages

steps.action: Unit test inspection: escape-html.test.ts 'neutralizes img onerror XSS payload'
steps.observed: Test asserts escapeHtml('') does not contain raw

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed. All OAuth callback routes properly escape HTML in user-controlled inputs via escapeHtml(). Both routes use the same handler (handleOpenAiBrowserCallback). The five HTML-significant characters are correctly entity-encoded, static strings pass through without artifacts, and the catch block path uses htmlError() which applies escapeHtml() to exception messages.
